data.title: As a developer, an E2E acceptance test verifies duplicates and supersedes relations end-to-end
data.description:
## Description

**What**: End-to-end acceptance test that exercises the duplicates and supersedes relations through the full stack: CLI -> event log -> reducer -> dep graph output.

**Why**: New relation values touch three layers (CLI validation, event writing, reducer replay). A single end-to-end test confirms all three compose correctly and prevents regressions.

**Scope**:
- IN: Test fixture creates three tickets A, B, C; links A duplicates B; links C supersedes A; verifies via ticket deps that relations are present with correct direction; verifies blockers and ready_to_work are unaffected; unlinks and verifies removal.
- IN: Test runs in an isolated .tickets-tracker fixture (DSO_TEST_FIXTURE=1 when Epic 1de7-50d4 guard lands; until then, runs in a scratch tracker dir).
- OUT: Jira bridge behavior — handled by Epic 83e2-04ae E2E tests.
- OUT: Closure-on-duplicate workflow — agents must transition explicitly.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, an acceptance test exercises the duplicates and supersedes link creation, reducer round-trip, blockers exclusion, ready_to_work unaffected, and unlink behavior end-to-end.
  ← Satisfies: "Add duplicates and supersedes as new link relation values"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic.

## Considerations

- [Testing] Must run in isolated fixture directory so it does not leak Test-authored tickets into the real tracker (guard lands in Epic 1de7-50d4).
- [Reliability] Test must include the idempotency case — issuing the same LINK twice is a no-op.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
